headline: Should Brown-Forman’s (BF.B) Revenue Beat but Softer EBITDA Shift Its Premiumization Growth Narrative?
summary: In its latest quarterly update, Brown-Forman reported past revenues of US$1.04 billion, a 5.4% decline year on year but still ahead of analyst expectations, alongside mixed profit metrics with adjusted operating income meeting forecasts while EBITDA missed. Management also reiterated a focus on premiumization and expanding flavored whiskey and ready-to-drink lines in resilient emerging markets, signaling how brand mix and geography remain central to the company’s long-term growth...
